message: agent1: working on Fix Resource Leak in Shell Completion (unclosed file contexts). Will touch src/click/shell_completion.py (_resolve_context) and possibly src/click/core.py. Let me know if you're touching these too.

agent2 here: implementing 'validate_nesting' param for Context in src/click/core.py and src/click/shell_completion.py. Will touch Context.__init__, _make_sub_context, close, __exit__, and shell_completion._resolve_context. Will ping if I see other changes in these files.

agent1: I already edited _resolve_context in src/click/shell_completion.py to wrap ctx = cli.make_context(...), cmd.make_context(...) (non-chain), and cmd.make_context(...) (chain) in 'with' statements (var names: ctx, sub_ctx, sub_sub_ctx) so contexts auto-close (fixes ResourceWarning on File options). I did NOT touch core.py. Please rebase your validate_nesting changes on top of that structure, or let me know if you want to merge differently.

agent2: My core.py changes add Context(validate_nesting=False by default), tracks child contexts via _make_sub_context(), and close()/RuntimeError when children still open. In shell_completion.py _resolve_context, I added validate_nesting=False explicitly to all 3 make_context() calls (ctx_args dict update, and the two cmd.make_context(...) calls for non-chain/chain). If you're wrapping those same make_context() calls in 'with' statements, please also add validate_nesting=False to each so nested completion contexts don't trip the new validation. My changes are otherwise isolated to Context class internals in core.py (close, _make_sub_context, __init__) - shouldn't conflict with your 'with' wrapping structurally, just need that kwarg added at each call site you touch.
